expected_behavior: Explains what the skill does and what AI-DLC means in this skill
Describes the four maturity levels, the three lifecycle stages, and people/process alignment
Explains the persistent Markdown assessment artifact workflow
Does not produce a maturity score or inline JSON because no context was provided
Lists the available usage patterns and modes
Does not run an assessment or produce a score
Explains the maturity model: the four levels and the three lifecycle stages
Lists the six review dimensions and the people/process dimension
Uses the renamed dimension names (for example Observability & Traceability, Execution Efficiency, Resource Stewardship) and never uses AWS Well-Architected terms or pillar names
Does not score a specific team
Returns assessment questions grouped by lifecycle, dimension, and people/process alignment
Groups questions under the six renamed dimensions, not AWS pillar names
Does not produce a maturity score
Lists evidence artifacts a team should collect before scoring, such as rule files, PR samples, CI logs, security scans, approvals, cost dashboards, incidents, and trace links
Frames the evidence as preparation that should precede a full assessment

query: /ai-dlc-maturity We are using AI in our SDLC. Are we doing it the right way?
expected_behavior: Creates a fillable Markdown assessment intake artifact based on the assessment artifact template
Covers scope, lifecycle coverage, people/process, engineering controls, traceability, security and governance, cost, evidence, and concerns
Does not fabricate a maturity score and waits for context or completed answers
If file write access is available, offers outputs/ai-dlc-maturity-assessment.md as the living artifact, otherwise shows it in the response

query: /ai-dlc-maturity Please prepare a Markdown assessment artifact for our platform team to fill out.
expected_behavior: Produces a reusable Markdown intake artifact
Explains how to continue the assessment after the artifact is filled
Preserves the intake structure so it can be reused and reviewed later

query: /ai-dlc-maturity Our team uses AI coding assistants for implementation and unit test drafting. Developers review PRs manually. CI runs tests and linting, but prompts and agent rules are personal, not versioned. We do not track token cost or trace shipped changes back to intent.
expected_behavior: Scores the team conservatively, treating claimed-but-unevidenced practices as partial evidence
Identifies people/process gaps, traceability gaps, and cost gaps
Recommends versioned rules, evidence capture, CI gates, and cost attribution
Outputs a readable maturity card followed by inline JSON that conforms to the schema, including dimension_scores with the six renamed dimension keys
Does not recommend named vendors, models, or products

query: Continue the AI-DLC maturity assessment from this completed intake: [filled Markdown answers]
expected_behavior: Preserves the original questions and answers in the artifact
Appends the maturity assessment, people/process findings, top gaps, priority actions, evidence gaps, assumptions, and inline JSON
Does not silently delete or overwrite the user's intake content

query: /ai-dlc-maturity --review Assess our AI-DLC practice. We use AI across requirements, coding, test generation, PR review, and release notes. We have CI gates but no standard audit schema for model identity, prompt version, rule version, approval, and linked tests.
expected_behavior: Frames the output as a structured maturity review
Organizes findings across the lifecycle stages and the six review dimensions
Never uses the term Well-Architected or AWS pillar names
Includes an overall maturity level and priority actions

query: /ai-dlc-maturity --brownfield We are applying AI to a large legacy system with poor test coverage and want to modernize it safely.
expected_behavior: Adds brownfield and legacy-system questions such as understanding-before-generation, characterization tests, and earned autonomy
Integrates the brownfield checks into the assessment rather than ignoring them
Scores conservatively given the legacy and low-test-coverage context

query: /ai-dlc-maturity --card-only Our team versions prompts and rules, runs CI gates on AI changes, and tracks cost by workflow.
expected_behavior: Returns only the readable maturity assessment card
Omits the inline JSON snapshot

query: /ai-dlc-maturity --json-only Our team versions prompts and rules, runs CI gates on AI changes, and tracks cost by workflow.
expected_behavior: Returns only the inline JSON snapshot in a fenced json block
The JSON conforms to the schema, including dimension_scores with the six renamed dimension keys
Omits the readable card
